title: host-asterinas-stale: jjf stale --days N — issues not touched in N days
body:
# Goal

`jjf stale --days N` — surface issues not touched in N days.
Beads-style hygiene query for orchestrators working a large
issue tree.

# Why now

`host-asterinas-audit` (1588625) identified this as one of two
beads primitives jjforge needs to host the 968-issue asterinas
tree day-to-day. With 71 InProgress tickets currently open in
the asterinas tree, identifying abandoned work matters for the
orchestrator's loop — and the data is already there (the
`updated_at` field on every issue).

# Sketch

## CLI

```
jjf stale [--days N] [--status <s>] [--label <l>] [--type <t>] \
          [--limit N] [--json]
```

Default `--days 14`. Filters compose AND-style.

## Output

Plain text: one line per stale issue, `id  age  title  status`.
JSON: `[{id, title, status, updated_at, days_since_update}]`.
Sort ascending by `updated_at` (oldest first).

## Storage

Trivially derived from the existing snapshot: walk every issue,
compute `now - updated_at`, filter `>= N days`. No new fields,
no new ops, no spec bump.

# Out of scope

- Auto-closing stale issues. (Hygiene query only.)
- Slack/email pings. (Out-of-band notification.)
- "Stale by activity" — comments don't bump `updated_at` today
  (only mutating verbs do); that's a separate decision tracked
  in the storage spec. This ticket uses the field as-is.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `jjf stale --days 14` returns issues with `updated_at`
      older than 14 days, oldest first.
- [ ] `--json` envelope returns `{ok: true, results: [...]}`.
- [ ] `--status` / `--label` / `--type` filters compose with
      the staleness threshold.
- [ ] Closed issues excluded by default; `--status closed`
      includes them.
- [ ] Tests cover the boundary day, the empty-result case, and
      filter composition.
